name: Tuberculosis Susceptibility
summary: Tuberculosis susceptibility and outcomes depend on exposure intensity and host immune response; host genetics evidence is heterogeneous across settings. GENARCH provides educational summaries only.
adolescent_relevance: TB affects adolescents/young adults in many regions; included for educational host genetics and exposure context only.

heritability_estimate.source: Estimates vary widely by exposure setting and phenotype definition

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS not broadly established; highly context-dependent.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: Crowding increases exposure opportunity for airborne pathogens.
Nutrition status relates to immune function; confounding is substantial.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: TB studies are region- and cohort-specific; ancestry and pathogen lineage context matters.
population_equity.transferability_notes: Host genetics may not transfer across settings with different exposure intensity.
population_equity.data_gaps: Phenotype definitions and exposure measurement vary widely.

references.title: Host genetics of tuberculosis susceptibility: overview (review)
Housing conditions and respiratory infection risk: overview (review)
Air pollution and health: evidence and mechanisms (review)
references.journal: Journal of the Air & Waste Management Association
